reviewer_prompt: Why is the multilingual package publishable when only one row is direct official?
fast_answer_now: Because LIMEN is using the multilingual slice as an evidence-maturity and observability-bias package, not as six equally verified cross-country incidents. The current reviewer-safe contract is 1 bounded substantive direct-official Slovenian row, 2 bounded non-official comparator rows, 3 queue-only limitations rows, and 6-of-6 translation-review-required rows.
paper_thesis_use: Fast reviewer-response paragraph; methods/results defense for Table 1D plus Figure 6; thesis methods appendix note.
dashboard_hook: Table 1D ladder; Figure 6 legend; multilingual evidence-maturity sidebar.
forbidden_overread: Do not rewrite the package as multilingual prevalence, country ranking, or six substantively validated cases.
next_threshold_event: Only a real row-level authority or visibility upgrade should change this answer; prose-only maintenance should not.

reviewer_prompt: Why do all six multilingual rows still carry translation-review-required metadata if three are direct-source-resolved?
fast_answer_now: Because direct-source resolution and translation finality are separate controls. Three rows are readable enough for bounded machine-assisted extraction, but none has yet cleared human translation review, so every row still requires explicit translation caution in paper, dashboard, and thesis reuse.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/limitations rebuttal; reproducibility note; thesis guardrail on machine translation.
forbidden_overread: Do not convert machine-assisted reading into final legal, policy, deployment, or criminal-process conclusions.
next_threshold_event: Human translation review can harden wording, but it should not widen claims unless it reveals a direct contradiction in source meaning.

reviewer_prompt: Why is Finland still caution-only instead of a substantive multilingual geography?
fast_answer_now: Because Finland is split across two different evidence states: Yle is a bounded broadcaster-source comparator with concrete institutional detail, while Kaleva remains queue-only at the stronger but still limited preview_text_visible_direct_article_shell_candidate state. Together they justify methods/limitations and mixed-authority discussion, not default substantive geography status.
paper_thesis_use: Finland caution-state rebuttal; methods note on mixed-authority geographies; thesis footnote on route discipline.
dashboard_hook: Finland caution badge; route-order tooltip; mixed-authority geography overlay.
forbidden_overread: Do not present Finland as official corroboration, default-visible substantive geography, or body-level confirmation of offense detail or chronology.
next_threshold_event: One exact Finnish official corroboration for Yle or one compliant Kaleva/public-companion recovery event remains the only real threshold-changing Finland move.

reviewer_prompt: Why keep Lithuania and Estonia visible if both are unresolved?
fast_answer_now: Because they are reviewer-useful negative evidence under the rare-language directive. Lithuania and Estonia show two different barrier classes in high-undercovered languages — mixed rights/access friction and challenge-gated retrieval — and the atlas would be methodologically weaker if those blocked routes disappeared from view.
paper_thesis_use: Rare-language methods defense; limitations paragraph; thesis note on under-covered-language friction.
dashboard_hook: Rare-language barrier map; blocker-class matrix; queue-only pressure panel.
forbidden_overread: Do not narrate Lithuania or Estonia as evidence of absence, validated incidents, or institutional failure findings.
next_threshold_event: A genuinely different compliant public route would be needed before either row can outrank Finland again.

reviewer_prompt: What changed when Kaleva moved from metadata-only recovery to preview_text_visible_direct_article_shell_candidate?
fast_answer_now: The change improved provenance and methods value, not substantive claim depth. LIMEN can now point to a browser-reachable direct article shell with visible title, date, byline, premium state, and bounded preview text confirming suspicion-stage framing, but still cannot claim full-body detail, offense chronology, anonymization, or legal posture from that source alone.
paper_thesis_use: Methods note on partial recovery states; queue-state rebuttal; thesis appendix note on preview-text-visible article-shell improvements.
dashboard_hook: Queue-depth bar; preview-text-visible-vs-title-only unresolved split; Finland tooltip.
forbidden_overread: Do not treat preview text as full body confirmation, legal validation, or permission to promote the row beyond queue-only limitations.
next_threshold_event: The next real gain is still one compliant public companion or readable-body recovery event, not another wording-only synchronization pass.

reviewer_prompt: What is the smallest multilingual move that matters more than another packaging pass?
fast_answer_now: Exactly one threshold-changing Finland event: either an official corroboration surface for Yle or a public-companion/body-readable upgrade for Kaleva. Under the current host-state evidence, those are the only near-term changes that would materially alter claim support, the 1/2/3 package split, or Figure 6 visibility logic.
paper_thesis_use: Reviewer-response closing row; route-selection note; thesis backlog governance snippet.
dashboard_hook: Priority ladder widget for threshold-changing moves versus packaging-only maintenance.
forbidden_overread: Do not spend another cycle on multilingual prose-only repackaging unless a real reviewer-facing drift bug reappears.
next_threshold_event: Prefer exactly one Finland threshold attempt next; otherwise switch effort to another LIMEN shard with a clearer empirical gain.
